relations.note: Metadata declaring background color allows the assembler to enforce the dark-background adjacency exclusion.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Ensure reliable modular alignment and consistent vertical rhythm across stacked email sections.
rules.rule_text: Each section must be structured as a self-contained table-based block with a fixed width of 600px (dimension.canvas.width = "600px"), utilizing 32px top/bottom padding (padding.section.vertical = "32px"), with zero outer margins to support direct stacking assembly.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Enable the automated assembler to validate content eligibility, claim assignments, and style transitions.
rules.rule_text: Every section metadata must explicitly declare its background color, if the section establishes the first mention of 'dermatomyositis (DM)', the footnote symbols consumed, and if a CTA button is present inside the section.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Prevent footnote collisions and misaligned references across dynamically stacked content sections.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols must be superscripted and assigned dynamically in sequence order during the assembly step. Raw daggers and footnote symbols must not be hardcoded when multiple referenced claims are present; placeholder tokens must be used instead.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Maintain design system integrity and prevent visual breakage around fixed structural templates.
rules.rule_text: Sections adjacent to locked core components (header, footer, ISI) must not contain decorative graphic elements that visually depend on the adjoining component's artwork.

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Avoid visual monotony and maintain clear, scannable section divisions in stacked layouts.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be positioned adjacent to other dark-background sections during email assembly.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Ensure robust, pixel-perfect rendering parity across legacy email clients like Outlook.
rules.rule_text: All HTML code must declare colors strictly as HEX, all sizing strictly in px, and use inline CSS only. The background color attribute (`bgcolor`) must be duplicated directly on table cells for Outlook compatibility.
